problem_solved: Provides comprehensive GP practice management and clinical IT systems for primary care. Enables integrated care across the NHS through electronic health records and data analytics.
who_buys: GP practices, primary care networks (PCNs), community pharmacies, hospitals, and NHS trusts
key_features: EMIS Web - cloud-based clinical system with EHR, appointments, and prescribing
Population health management and analytics for proactive care
Interoperability across primary, community, and secondary care settings
business_model: SaaS subscription model with per-practice/per-user licensing; acquired by Optum (UnitedHealth) in 2023
why_varden_should_care: Deep NHS integration creates high switching costs. Model of combining clinical workflows with analytics is replicable in fragmented markets. Proven path from practice management to population health platform.

problem_solved: Provides a unified clinical system (SystmOne) that makes electronic health records securely available across the NHS. Connects care across 25+ settings from GP practices to hospitals.
who_buys: GP practices, NHS organizations, community care providers, hospitals, and specialist clinics
key_features: SystmOne - single database EHR with 61 million patient records
Shared care records accessible across 7,800+ NHS organizations
Patient-facing app for appointment booking and health records access
business_model: SaaS/subscription model with centralized database architecture; per-organization licensing
why_varden_should_care: Single-database architecture demonstrates scalability and data consistency advantages. Proves that 'boring' EHR infrastructure can capture massive market share through simplicity and integration.

problem_solved: Provides digital-first primary care through video consultations, chat, and physical clinics. Reduces barriers to accessing GP care and decreases pressure on emergency services.
who_buys: Individual patients (B2C via regional healthcare subsidies), regional health authorities, employers (B2B occupational health)
key_features: 24/7 digital consultations via app with doctors, psychologists, and nurses
Hybrid model with 34+ physical clinics across Sweden
Integrated care from digital triage to in-person visits and specialist referrals
business_model: Hybrid B2C (patient fees subsidized by regional healthcare) and B2B (employer contracts); SaaS platform for care delivery
why_varden_should_care: Digital-first approach to primary care reduces overhead and increases accessibility. Hybrid model proves telemedicine alone isn't enough - physical presence matters. Strong replicability in regions with healthcare access challenges.

problem_solved: Provides healthcare IT solutions, practice management software, and data services for healthcare professionals, insurers, and life sciences companies across Europe.
who_buys: Pharmacies, GP practices, specialists, health insurance companies, pharmaceutical companies, and healthcare administrators
key_features: Practice management and dispensing software for pharmacies and clinics
Healthcare data and analytics platform for life sciences and insurers
Claims processing and dematerialized exchange services
business_model: SaaS and transaction-based model; software licensing, data services, and BPO for healthcare payers and providers
why_varden_should_care: Multi-stakeholder approach creates network effects across providers, payers, and pharma. Proves that healthcare data platforms can serve multiple customer segments. 50+ years of experience shows sustainability of boring infrastructure plays.

hq_location: Koblenz, Germany (with major operations in Stockholm, Sweden)
problem_solved: Provides comprehensive medical practice management systems and e-health solutions for healthcare providers across Europe. Enables secure, connected care through digital health records.
who_buys: GP practices, specialist clinics, corporate health services, pediatric care, laboratories, schools, and home healthcare providers
key_features: CGM J4 - comprehensive journal/EHR system with 85,000+ users in Sweden
Telemedicine and video consultation platform (CGM Video)
Integration with regional and national health information exchanges
business_model: SaaS subscription model with per-organization licensing; 40+ years experience in healthcare IT
why_varden_should_care: Demonstrates successful cross-border expansion (German HQ, Swedish stronghold). Shows that long-term relationships with healthcare systems create defensible positions. Focus on making 'health information available when needed' is core infrastructure thesis.

problem_solved: Provides 24/7 telemedicine services and digital health platform for Swiss residents. Reduces unnecessary emergency visits and improves healthcare accessibility through digital-first triage and treatment.
who_buys: Health insurance companies (white-label solutions), individual patients (covered by mandatory Swiss health insurance), employers (occupational health)
key_features: 24/7 telemedicine consultations via phone, video, and chat with 160+ physicians
Symptom checker and digital triage in mobile app
Integration with 3,800+ specialist network for referrals and physical care
business_model: B2B2C through insurance partnerships and direct patient billing via Swiss health insurance; per-consultation fees covered by basic insurance
why_varden_should_care: Insurance-integrated model creates sustainable revenue and reduces patient cost barriers. Proves telemedicine can handle emergencies (Kids Line) and reduce ED burden. High patient satisfaction (95%) shows quality doesn't require physical presence.

problem_solved: Provides patient acquisition and practice management software for healthcare providers. Connects patients with doctors and helps clinics manage appointments, reduce no-shows, and build online reputation.
who_buys: Individual doctors, multi-specialty clinics, and healthcare systems across 13 countries
key_features: Patient booking platform with 25M+ monthly appointments across 13 countries
Practice management software with scheduling, patient CRM, and analytics
Doctor review and reputation management system
business_model: Marketplace model (freemium for doctors) + SaaS subscription for practice management tools; operates country-specific brands (Znanylekarz.pl, Doctoralia, MioDottore, etc.)
why_varden_should_care: Platform approach combines patient acquisition (marketplace) with retention tools (practice management). Multi-brand, multi-country strategy shows how to scale across fragmented European markets. 300K active doctors demonstrates network effect potential.

problem_solved: Provides video consultation platform connecting patients with UK-registered GPs. Enables quick access to primary care consultations and prescriptions without visiting a physical practice.
who_buys: NHS patients (through GP practice partnerships), private patients (self-pay), and employers (occupational health services)
key_features: Video consultations with UK GPs via smartphone app
Digital prescription services and referrals
Integration with NHS GP practices for registered patients
business_model: B2B2C (NHS partnerships) and B2C (private pay) model; acquired by Square Health for corporate health services
why_varden_should_care: Early mover in UK telemedicine shows both opportunities and challenges of digital-first GP models. NHS integration demonstrates path to scale in public healthcare systems. Pivot to corporate/B2B shows business model evolution in response to market dynamics.
